free_text: During tonight's drill session, I noticed Cadet Smith seemed a bit left out when the section was choosing partners for the map reading exercise. A couple of the other cadets made comments about him being 'rubbish at navigation' and he ended up working alone.

Cadet Jones mentioned to me after parade that some of the older cadets in her section have been calling her 'baby cadet' and laughing when she gets drill movements wrong. She said it's been happening for about a week now.

I observed two cadets, Williams and Thompson, having a disagreement over who would be section commander during the field exercise. Williams pushed Thompson lightly on the shoulder and told him to 'get lost'. Thompson walked away looking annoyed.

Cadet Brown approached me before evening meal and said that Cadet Taylor keeps making jokes about her accent in front of the other cadets. She said it's starting to make her feel uncomfortable but it's not really serious.

During free time, I noticed Cadet Davies sitting by himself while his usual group of friends were playing cards together. When I asked if he wanted to join in, one of the cadets said 'Davies is being moody today' and the others giggled.

During the drill practice, I noticed Cadet Smith making faces and mimicking Cadet Jones when he was trying to follow commands. A couple of other cadets were giggling at this. Cadet Jones didn't seem to notice but looked a bit flustered when he couldn't get the movements right.

Cadet Williams told me that some of the older cadets keep calling her 'short stuff' and making jokes about her height. She said it's not really mean but it's getting a bit annoying. She mentioned it happens mostly during break times.

I observed Cadet Thompson deliberately moving away from Cadet Clarke during team activities today. When I asked Thompson about it later, he said Clarke had been copying his answers during the navigation exercise and it wasn't fair.

Two cadets, Miller and Davies, had a brief argument about who should carry the flag during parade practice. Miller said Davies always gets picked for everything and it's not fair. They sorted it out themselves but there was some tension between them for the rest of the session.

Cadet Roberts mentioned that her friend from another section has been ignoring her messages on social media since last week's camp. She said they had a small disagreement about room arrangements but doesn't understand why her friend is still upset.

Cadet Smith mentioned that some of the older cadets have been calling him names during drill practice. He said they've made comments about his height a couple of times this week. He seemed a bit embarrassed when telling me but said it wasn't a big deal.

Noticed Cadet Jones sitting alone at lunch break today. When I asked if everything was okay, she said that her usual friend group had been leaving her out of conversations lately. She mentioned feeling a bit left out but said she was managing fine.

Cadet Williams approached me after parade to say that Cadet Brown had been making jokes about his accent in front of others. He said it happened twice this month and made him feel uncomfortable. He wasn't sure if he should say anything about it.

During fieldcraft training, I overheard Cadet Davis telling another cadet that some of the lads had been teasing him about his new glasses. He said they'd been calling him 'four eyes' for the past few days. He laughed it off but looked a bit fed up.

Cadet Thompson mentioned that she'd had a small disagreement with her section partner about sharing equipment. She said the other cadet had been a bit snappy with her recently and they weren't getting along as well as usual.

Cadet Williams mentioned to me after parade that some of the older cadets in her section keep calling her 'newbie' and laughing when she gets drill movements wrong. She said it's been happening for the past two weeks but seemed more frustrated than upset about it.

I observed Cadet Thompson rolling his eyes and making quiet comments to his mate whenever Cadet Harris answered questions during the first aid lesson. Harris didn't seem to notice but it happened several times throughout the session.

Cadet Price approached me after the social evening to say that two cadets from another section had been making jokes about his accent during break time. He said they weren't being mean exactly but it was starting to annoy him.

During fieldcraft training I noticed that when Cadet Matthews made a mistake with her camouflage, a couple of the lads in her patrol started giggling and whispering to each other. She looked a bit embarrassed but carried on with the exercise.

During drill practice today, I noticed Cadet Smith rolling his eyes and making quiet comments when Cadet Jones was giving commands. A couple of other cadets seemed to find it amusing. Cadet Jones didn't appear to notice but looked a bit flustered during the exercise.

Cadet Williams mentioned that some of the older cadets in his section have been calling him 'baby face' and laughing about his voice breaking. He said it happens most weeks but seemed more annoyed than upset about it.

Observed Cadet Thompson deliberately walking away when Cadet Brown tried to join the group during break time. Cadet Brown stood alone for a few minutes before joining a different group. This is the second time I've seen similar behaviour.

Cadet Davies told me that Cadet Miller keeps making jokes about his trainers being 'cheap knock-offs' in front of others. He said it's been going on for about three weeks and it's getting on his nerves.

During team activities, Cadet Green consistently chooses teams that exclude Cadet Taylor. When Cadet Taylor was eventually picked for a team, Cadet Green made a comment about 'having to carry dead weight' which caused some snickering from others.

Cadet Smith approached me after parade and said that three older cadets have been calling him names and deliberately excluding him from group activities for the past month. He looked upset and said it's been happening every week during fieldcraft training. He specifically mentioned they call him 'useless' and won't let him join their section when we split into teams.

During the break, I noticed Cadet Jones sitting alone looking distressed. When I asked if everything was okay, she told me that Cadet Williams has been sending her nasty messages on social media calling her fat and stupid. She showed me screenshots on her phone from the past two weeks with multiple cruel comments about her appearance and abilities.

Cadet Thompson's mum contacted me saying her son doesn't want to come to cadets anymore because some of the other cadets have been picking on him. She said he's been coming home upset for three weeks now and yesterday he was in tears saying they push him around and hide his kit. She's worried about his confidence as he used to love coming.

Other cadets nearby were also laughing. Cadet Clark looked embarrassed and I noticed this is the third time I've seen similar behaviour from Davies towards Clark over recent weeks.

I observed a pattern where Cadet Martinez is consistently left out when cadets form groups for activities, and I've heard sniggers when he volunteers answers during lessons. Several cadets reported that Marcus has been deliberately damaging other people's kit during field exercises. Last weekend he allegedly put mud in another cadet's boots and hid someone's beret. Two cadets said they're now afraid to leave their belongings unattended when Marcus is around.

Chloe came to see me upset because a group of cadets have been spreading rumours that she's been 'getting too friendly' with one of the adult instructors. She said the comments started after she received a commendation last month and now she feels uncomfortable participating in activities.

Cadet Williams disclosed that three older cadets have been forcing him to hand over his lunch money every week for the past month. He said they threatened to beat him up if he tells anyone and showed me bruising on his arm which he said was from when he initially refused to pay them.

During tonight's parade, Cadet Thompson broke down crying and told me that Cadet Jones and his friends have been sending her explicit photos and videos via WhatsApp, demanding she send similar content back or they'll share embarrassing photos of her around the unit.
